rag_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes with visible feature names.
Generate AGREE annex blocks using valid syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, assign.
generic_rules: Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and expressions; use int, real, bool for AGREE types.
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in output dependency guarantees.
Avoid referencing AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Do not invent new identifiers; use only visible symbols from the current component scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations.
Implementation annexes are for assignments and bindings.
Use logical operators (and, or, not, =>) in AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL timing properties (e.g., Deadline, Period) as AGREE variables.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not generate clauses with unresolved identifiers or mismatched types.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available in AGREE scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints
Model fusion and component insertion locations
